title: Liveness probe never triggers
body:
### Issue Description

I'm using a k8s definition file to start an API server container and I have liveness probe added to the file as below:

```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: api-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: api-container
    image: localhost/api:latest
    ports:
    - containerPort: 8000
    volumeMounts:
    - name: models-files
      mountPath: /root/models-files
    command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
    args: ["pip install --upgrade pip && pip install --no-cache-dir -r /root/api/requirements.txt && cd /root/api/ && uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000"]
    livenessProbe:
      exec:
        command: ["bash", "-c", "/usr/local/bin/health-check.sh"]
      initialDelaySeconds: 300
      periodSeconds: 300
  volumes:
  - name: models-files
    hostPath:
      path: /home/guney/models-files
      type: Directory
```

I need to use a script for health check because I also want to record some values to database on every interval. But it's either returning `exit 1` or `exit 0` at the end depending on the check.

When I run `podman kube play <yamlFile>` container starts and says `Up <number> minutes (starting)`. The problem is, the liveness probe never starts to trigger depending on the values you put to the liveness probe and status of the container always stays as `starting`.

If I run `podman healthcheck run <containerName>` then it knows about my healthcheck script, it runs it, it records data to DB and also it says `healthy` in container status. But still this will not help it to keep triggering let say every 5 Minutes.

I checked you test yaml files in `/test/e2e/play_kube_test.go` and also tested with these liveness probe yaml files locally. Same behavior still goes on and interval never triggers a healthcheck.

Then I checked the test code for these yaml files provided in the `/test/e2e/play_kube_test.go`, basically they are not testing if these probes are running, but instead it's testing if these probes are there or running them with `podman healthcheck run <containerName>` command and see if the state of the container changes. None of these tests are checking if healthchecks are getting triggered by the service and apparently they are not.

Here are the tests:

```
	It("podman play kube liveness probe should fail", func() {
		err := writeYaml(livenessProbeUnhealthyPodYaml, kubeYaml)
		Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())

		kube := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"play", "kube", kubeYaml})
		kube.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
		Expect(kube).Should(Exit(0))

		time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
		hc := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"healthcheck", "run", "liveness-unhealthy-probe-pod-alpine"})
		hc.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
		hcoutput := hc.OutputToString()
		Expect(hcoutput).To(ContainSubstring(define.HealthCheckUnhealthy))
	})

	It("podman play kube support container startup probe", func() {
		ctrName := "startup-healthy-probe-pod-alpine"
		err := writeYaml(startupProbePodYaml, kubeYaml)
		Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred())

		kube := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"play", "kube", kubeYaml})
		kube.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
		Expect(kube).Should(Exit(0))

		time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
		inspect := podmanTest.InspectContainer(ctrName)
		Expect(inspect[0].State.Health).To(HaveField("Status", "starting"))

		hc := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"healthcheck", "run", ctrName})
		hc.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
		Expect(hc).Should(Exit(1))

		exec := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"exec", ctrName, "sh", "-c", "echo 'startup probe success' > /testfile"})
		exec.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
		Expect(exec).Should(Exit(0))

		hc = podmanTest.Podman([]string{"healthcheck", "run", ctrName})
		hc.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
		Expect(hc).Should(Exit(0))

		inspect = podmanTest.InspectContainer(ctrName)
		Expect(inspect[0].State.Health).To(HaveField("Status", define.HealthCheckHealthy))
	})
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a container from k8s definition file with `liveness probe` exists
2. See if the `liveness probe` is gonna get triggered to handle `podman healthcheck run <containerName` command by itself.
3. See if the `state` of the container is gonna change.


### Describe the results you received

Liveness probe implementation in podman creates healthcheck commands and status but never triggers intervaled healthcheck but instead you should run `podman healthcheck run <containerName>` command to trigger it.

### Describe the results you expected

Liveness probes are having `periodSeconds` and `initialDelaySeconds` which these are values to tell the service about triggers so that no extra `cron job` or something to be created, this liveness probe should have trigger healthchecks by itself.

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I tried this out with a simple k8s yaml and latest podman:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    test: liveness
  name: liveness-exec
spec:
  containers:
  - name: liveness
    image: registry.k8s.io/busybox
    args:
    - /bin/sh
    - -c
    - touch /tmp/healthy; sleep 30; rm -f /tmp/healthy; sleep 600
    livenessProbe:
      exec:
        command:
        - cat
        - /tmp/healthy
      initialDelaySeconds: 5
      periodSeconds: 5
```

And the liveness probe check works as expected, initially my container reports `healthy` while the `/tmp/healthy` file exists and after it is removed, the status changes to `unhealthy`

```
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                        COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS                   PORTS       NAMES
3e683931e7a5  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.0-dev-1695057404                        24 seconds ago  Up 24 seconds                        5af5598842b5-infra
9a61b34b3bc7  registry.k8s.io/busybox:latest               /bin/sh -c touch ...  24 seconds ago  Up 24 seconds (healthy)              liveness-exec-liveness

$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                        COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS                     PORTS       NAMES
3e683931e7a5  localhost/podman-pause:4.7.0-dev-1695057404                        50 seconds ago  Up 50 seconds                          5af5598842b5-infra
9a61b34b3bc7  registry.k8s.io/busybox:latest               /bin/sh -c touch ...  50 seconds ago  Up 50 seconds (unhealthy)              liveness-exec-liveness
```

I see you are on podman 4.5.1, can you try it out with latest podman available, either 4.6.x or upstream main. I will take a look at the tests and update them where needed.

Can you also please double check that the liveness probe script that the container is expected to have is actually there.

Thanks for the answer,
I'll try with another one of the newer podman versions to see if that works.
Yeah I'm pretty sure my script is there and it works because, since podman liveness probe never triggers for me, I had to create a cronjob that runs `podman healthcheck  run <containerID>` command to make it work.
And after I do that container healtch state changes accordingly depending on the script result.
